                mod_map[current_mod] = current_value

    prefix_sum - dp
end

But in JavaScript, we can use a Map for modMap. However, for efficiency, perhaps using an array is better, but since k can be up to 1e5, using a Map is acceptable.

function solve(nums, k) {

    let prefixSum = 0;

    let dp = 0;

    let modMap = new Map();

    modMap.set(0, 0);

    for (const num of nums) {

        prefixSum += num;

        const currentMod = prefixSum % k;

        const maxPrev = modMap.get(currentMod) ?? -Infinity;

        const candidate = prefixSum + maxPrev;

        dp = Math.max(dp, candidate);

        const currentKey = currentMod;

        const currentValue = dp - prefixSum;

        if (modMap.has(currentKey)) {

            if (currentValue > modMap.get(currentKey)) {

                modMap.set(currentKey, currentValue);

            }

        } else {

            modMap.set(currentKey, currentValue);

        }

    }

    return prefixSum - dp;

}
